opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The cramps are so violent I can't stand up, and I can't keep a drop of water down because I keep vomiting from the pain.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Agonising period cramps with vomiting started with my period and I can't keep fluids down.

symptom_narrative.onset: 6 hours ago, exactly as my period started
symptom_narrative.character: severe cramping suprapubic pain radiating to back and thighs, nausea/vomiting, malaise, mild diarrhoea
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: can't stand, vomiting from the pain, can't keep water down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: lower central abdomen, into my back and thighs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: with the first days of my period
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: nausea/vomiting, mild diarrhoea; symptoms began ~12 months after my first period and are cyclical; NO fever, no abnormal vaginal discharge, no pain with sex, no urinary symptoms; not sure if I could be pregnant
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: cyclical period pain, but this is exceptionally severe

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; can't retain fluids; pregnancy status not confirmed

objective_data_offered.value: severe central lower-abdominal cramps radiating to back/thighs, with the period
vomiting, can't keep fluids down; small amounts of clear/bile vomit
